---
title: "Regression II"
subtitle: "Non-linear regression, splines, and the kernel trick"
date: "Sep 18, 2026"
date-format: long
---

Polynomials, step functions, regression splines, smoothing splines, and an introduction to the kernel trick — moving beyond the linear model while keeping the regression framework.

📖 **Assigned reading:** ISLR ch. 7

---

## 📊 Slides

- 🖥️ [Clean slides](slides_clean.pdf)
- ✏️ [Annotated slides](slides_annotated.pdf)  *(in-class version)*
- 🖨️ [Slides (2-up)](slides_split.pdf)  *(two per page, for printing)*

## 📚 Readings

- 📄 [Stricker et al. (2017)](readings/Stricker et al. (2017).pdf)
- 📄 [Zimbalist et al. (2025)](readings/Zimbalist et al. (2025).pdf)

## 🧪 Lab

In-class walkthrough — open the rendered lab in a new tab. The R Markdown source is provided so you can run the code yourself in RStudio.

- 📝 [Lab source (lab1.R)](code/lab1.R)
- 📝 [Lab source (lab2.R)](code/lab2.R)

## 💻 Code

**Figure scripts** — reproduce each figure from the slides:

- 📈 [fig1.R](code/fig1.R)
- 📈 [fig2.R](code/fig2.R)
- 📈 [fig3.R](code/fig3.R)
- 📈 [fig4.R](code/fig4.R)
- 📈 [fig5.R](code/fig5.R)

**Other scripts:**

- 📜 [helper.R](code/helper.R)

## 🗂️ Data

- 📊 [diabetes.csv](data/diabetes.csv)
- 📊 [wages.csv](data/wages.csv)
